question_text: A company promotes its existing Sales Manager to the position of Vice President (Marketing). This is an example of:
explanation: Promotion is an internal source of recruitment where an employee is moved to a higher position with greater responsibilities, status, and pay. Here the Sales Manager is elevated to VP — a clear case of promotion.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Transfer is movement at the same level; here the position is higher, so it's promotion.
Direct recruitment involves hiring from outside at factory gate — this is internal.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing internal promotion with external recruitment

elimination_hints.hint: Campus placement involves visiting educational institutions — this is purely internal.

question_text: A factory puts up a notice at its gate: 'Wanted — 10 fitters. Walk-in between 9 AM to 12 PM.' This is an example of:
explanation: Direct recruitment (also called recruitment at factory gate) involves putting up notices at the enterprise gate specifying details of jobs available. Workers can directly walk in and apply. It is an external source used for filling casual or lower-level vacancies.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Employment exchanges are government agencies — this notice is at the factory gate directly.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing factory gate notice with employment exchange

elimination_hints.hint: Campus placement is at educational institutions, not at the factory gate.
Transfer involves existing employees — these are new walk-in applicants.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing external recruitment with internal transfer

question_text: In which selection test is a candidate asked to organise an imaginary in-tray of letters, memos, and reports?
explanation: An interest test measures the pattern of interests or involvement of a person in different activities. However, the in-tray/in-basket test is a type of simulation or situational test where candidates handle a set of papers, memos, and reports that would typically land on a manager's desk.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Aptitude test measures potential to learn — it doesn't simulate a real work scenario.
Intelligence test measures IQ and reasoning ability, not practical work simulation.
Personality test assesses emotional traits — it doesn't involve handling office paperwork.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing personality test with situational exercise

question_text: An IT company sends its team leaders to IIM Ahmedabad for a two-week management programme. This is an example of:
explanation: Off-the-job training methods include attending programmes at management institutes. Sending employees to IIM for a management programme takes them away from the workplace, making it an off-the-job development method.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: Vestibule training in a simulated environment
Apprenticeship programme under a master worker

elimination_hints.hint: Job rotation means moving through different departments within the company, not attending an external programme.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing external programme with job rotation

elimination_hints.hint: Vestibule training involves a simulated workplace setup — not attending an external management institute.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing management education with vestibule training

elimination_hints.hint: Apprenticeship involves learning a trade under a master worker — this is an executive development programme.

question_text: A new engineer at Tata Motors is asked to work in the Production department for 3 months, then Quality Control for 3 months, and then Supply Chain for 3 months. This is an example of:
explanation: Job rotation is an on-the-job training method where an employee is moved from one job to another at regular intervals. It broadens the employee's knowledge and skills across different functional areas.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Internship is for students/trainees, not for already-hired engineers of the company.
Vestibule training uses a simulated setup — this person works in actual departments.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing actual department rotation with simulated environment

elimination_hints.hint: Coaching involves a senior guiding a junior in one role — here the person moves across departments.

question_text: The purpose of a preliminary screening in the selection process is to:
explanation: Preliminary screening is the first step in the selection process. Its purpose is to quickly eliminate obviously unqualified or unsuitable candidates based on basic criteria (qualifications, experience, age) before investing time in detailed tests and interviews.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: Eliminate obviously unqualified candidates at an early stage
Test the technical skills of candidates in detail
Negotiate salary and other terms of employment
Verify the medical fitness of candidates

elimination_hints.hint: Detailed testing of technical skills happens in selection tests, not preliminary screening.
Salary negotiation happens near the end, after the selection decision is made.
elimination_hints.misconception: Placing salary discussion at screening stage

elimination_hints.hint: Medical examination comes near the end of the selection process, not at screening.
elimination_hints.misconception: Placing medical check at screening stage

question_text: Which of the following is NOT a type of selection test?
explanation: Placement test is not a standard type of selection test. The recognised types include intelligence tests, aptitude tests, personality tests, trade tests, and interest tests. Placement refers to putting the selected employee on the appropriate job after selection.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Intelligence test (IQ test) is a standard selection test measuring mental ability.
elimination_hints.misconception: Thinking intelligence test is not a selection test

elimination_hints.hint: Aptitude test measures potential to learn — it is a recognised selection test.
elimination_hints.misconception: Thinking aptitude test is not a selection test

elimination_hints.hint: Trade test measures existing skills/proficiency in a specific trade — it is a valid selection test.
elimination_hints.misconception: Thinking trade test is not a selection test

question_text: Staffing is important because:
explanation: Staffing helps in discovering and obtaining competent personnel for various positions. By ensuring the right people are in the right jobs, it directly contributes to productive performance and organisational success.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: It eliminates the need for any other management function
It helps in obtaining competent people for various jobs, leading to better performance
It automatically increases the profits of an organisation
It removes all conflicts between management and employees

elimination_hints.hint: No single function eliminates the need for others — all management functions work together.
Staffing improves performance but does not automatically guarantee higher profits.
Staffing helps but cannot remove all conflicts — some arise from other factors.

question_text: Labour market conditions refer to:
explanation: Labour market conditions refer to the supply and demand for labour in the market. When there is a surplus of qualified workers, recruitment is easier. When there is a shortage, organisations must offer better terms to attract talent.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: Supply and demand for qualified workers in the job market
The physical working conditions inside a factory
The machinery and technology used in production

elimination_hints.hint: Government rules about unions relate to industrial relations, not labour market conditions.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing market conditions with legal regulations

elimination_hints.hint: Physical working conditions inside a factory are workplace environment, not market conditions.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing internal conditions with market conditions

elimination_hints.hint: Machinery and technology relate to production, not the labour market.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing production factors with labour market

question_text: Coaching as a method of on-the-job training involves:
explanation: Coaching involves a superior guiding and instructing the trainee as a coach. The trainee works under the direct supervision of the senior, who provides feedback, suggestions, and guidance to improve performance.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: Moving the trainee through various departments periodically
Training in a simulated setup away from the workplace
A superior guiding and instructing the trainee at the workplace
Sending the trainee to a management institute for a course

elimination_hints.hint: Moving through departments is job rotation, not coaching.
Simulated setup away from the workplace describes vestibule training.
Sending to a management institute is an off-the-job method, not coaching.

question_text: Which of the following external sources of recruitment is most suitable for hiring fresh engineering graduates?
explanation: Campus recruitment (campus placement) involves companies visiting engineering and management colleges to recruit fresh graduates directly from the campus before they graduate. It is the most suitable method for hiring fresh graduates.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Employment exchanges are typically used for lower-level or skilled worker positions.
elimination_hints.misconception: Thinking exchanges are best for engineers

elimination_hints.hint: Labour contractors supply unskilled/semi-skilled workers, not engineering graduates.
Casual callers are walk-in applicants — not a targeted approach for fresh graduates.
elimination_hints.misconception: Thinking casual callers suit graduate recruitment

question_text: The employment interview in the selection process serves to:
explanation: The employment interview allows the employer to assess qualities like communication skills, confidence, body language, and overall suitability that cannot be judged through written tests alone. It also allows the candidate to learn about the organisation.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: Test the candidate's physical fitness for the job
Assess personality, communication skills, and overall suitability beyond written tests

elimination_hints.hint: Physical fitness is assessed through medical examination, not the interview.
Certificate verification happens during reference/background checks, not the interview.
Salary negotiation typically happens after the selection decision, not during the interview.
elimination_hints.misconception: Placing salary discussion in the interview stage

question_text: An advantage of external sources of recruitment is:
explanation: External recruitment brings in candidates with fresh perspectives, new ideas, and diverse experiences from other organisations. This helps in avoiding stagnation and inbreeding that can occur with only internal recruitment.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: It brings fresh talent with new ideas and diverse experience
It is less expensive than internal recruitment
Existing employees feel motivated and rewarded
The adjustment period for new employees is very short

elimination_hints.hint: External recruitment is usually more expensive due to advertising, testing, and onboarding costs.
elimination_hints.misconception: Thinking external is cheaper than internal

elimination_hints.hint: External recruitment may actually demotivate existing employees who expected promotion.
elimination_hints.misconception: Attributing internal source advantage to external

elimination_hints.hint: New external hires typically need longer to adjust than internal candidates who already know the organisation.

question_text: A limitation of using only internal sources of recruitment is:
explanation: Relying only on internal sources limits the choice to existing employees, which can lead to inbreeding. The organisation misses out on fresh talent and new ideas from outside, and there may not be a suitable internal candidate for specialised positions.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: It is very costly and time-consuming
It may lead to inbreeding and limited fresh perspectives
New employees take a long time to adjust

elimination_hints.hint: Internal recruitment is usually cheaper and faster — cost is a limitation of external sources.
Internal recruitment actually motivates employees — dissatisfaction is caused by external recruitment.
Internal candidates already know the organisation — adjustment time is for external hires.
elimination_hints.misconception: Attributing external source problem to internal

question_text: An interest test in the selection process measures:
explanation: An interest test identifies the areas of work in which a candidate shows special concern, involvement, or curiosity. It helps in selecting candidates for jobs that match their interests, leading to higher job satisfaction and performance.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: The candidate's IQ and reasoning ability
The candidate's pattern of involvement and preferences for certain activities
The candidate's existing proficiency in a particular trade
The candidate's emotional stability and temperament

elimination_hints.hint: IQ and reasoning are measured by intelligence tests, not interest tests.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing interest test with intelligence test

elimination_hints.hint: Existing trade proficiency is measured by trade tests.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing interest test with trade test

elimination_hints.hint: Emotional stability and temperament are assessed by personality tests.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing interest test with personality test

question_text: Internship training is a joint programme between:
explanation: Internship training is a joint programme of training in which educational institutions and business firms cooperate. Students work in organisations for a prescribed period to gain practical experience alongside their theoretical studies.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: An employer and a labour union

elimination_hints.hint: Competing firms would not jointly train employees — internship involves colleges and companies.
Internship is not a union-employer programme — it bridges academia and industry.
While government institutions can participate, the key partnership is between educational institutions and businesses.

question_text: Placement refers to:
explanation: Placement is the process of putting the selected candidate on the right job for which they have been selected. It involves assigning duties and responsibilities to the new employee based on their qualifications and the requirements of the position.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: Assigning the selected candidate to the job they have been hired for
Advertising a job vacancy through newspapers
Transferring an employee to a different department

elimination_hints.hint: Advertising vacancies is part of recruitment, not placement.
Written tests are part of the selection process, not placement.
Transfer is an internal source of recruitment — placement is the initial job assignment after selection.

question_text: Staffing as a function of management has gained importance in recent years because:
explanation: With advancement in technology and increasing size and complexity of business, the need for competent and well-trained staff has increased manifold. Human resources are the most important asset, making staffing a critical function.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: Machines have completely replaced human workers in all industries
Government has made it mandatory for all companies to have an HR department
Technology and business complexity have increased the need for competent personnel
Recruitment has become cheaper due to social media

elimination_hints.hint: Machines have not replaced humans completely — skilled personnel are needed to operate and manage technology.
There is no such universal government mandate — importance stems from business needs.
While social media helps, the importance of staffing is due to growing complexity, not just cheaper recruitment.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing recruitment cost with staffing importance

question_text: In the context of staffing, 'Human Resource Management' includes which of the following activities?
explanation: HRM encompasses manpower planning, recruitment, selection, placement, training, development, appraisal, remuneration, and maintaining good working conditions. It covers the complete lifecycle of an employee in the organisation.
subject: cuet-business-studies

options.text: Recruitment, selection, training, development, appraisal, and compensation collectively

elimination_hints.hint: HRM goes far beyond just recruitment and selection — it covers the entire employee lifecycle.
Training and development are part of HRM but don't cover the full scope.
Appraisal and compensation are components, but HRM includes many other activities too.
elimination_hints.misconception: Limiting HRM to appraisal and pay

question_text: Ravi, a production supervisor, is asked to temporarily handle the work of the absent quality supervisor without a change in pay or position. This is an example of:
explanation: Transfer involves moving an employee from one job to another, one department to another, or one shift to another without a substantive change in responsibilities, status, or pay. Here Ravi is temporarily moved to another department at the same level.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: There is no change in pay or position — promotion involves moving to a higher level.
elimination_hints.misconception: Thinking any job change is promotion

elimination_hints.hint: Direct recruitment brings in people from outside — Ravi is already an employee.
Job rotation is a planned training activity — this is a temporary fill-in for an absent colleague.
